mentions.note: Handheld ultrasound imaging (portable MRI replacement); no current earnings but very promising tech. Buy some at 8, lower if possible; very interesting long-term spec.
Recent quarter not blowout; stock hit on expectation reset despite recent stock split and strong fundamentals—contrarian buy.
Junior growth enthusiast opportunity; for those willing to take risk, position initiation after 5.6% rotation decline warranted.
Volatile pattern of undeserved declines followed by reversals. Stock lacks conviction support; pattern too exhausting for ownership.
Long-term member trust vehicle; ranges 800–1,000 with periodic compression to 45x earnings (vs. current 46x). When P/E drops to 45x, major move up likely; hold for catalyst.

Pure-play neocloud; $100B backlog visibility. Revenue ramp $13B→$25B→$40B (2026–2028); 2028 EPS $3.36, 2029 $6.92. Key risk: backlog-to-profit conversion post-debt/power. Cheaper than Nvidia's last stake price.
Stock on fire; execution remains strong. Management history of extreme conservative guidance on calls typically damps stock—upside likely remains.
At 17x 2030 earnings (est. $20 EPS), valuation similar to prior $100 recommendation despite $1,000 share price. Upside to 1,700 possible without becoming expensive; hold for travel trust.
Talc litigation now manageable; pure pharma play with strong oncology franchise. July 15 earnings catalyst; rotation dislocation creates entry below 256.
Nvidia $2B stake; Meta 5-year $27B deal (12B dedicated + 15B additional from 2027). Microsoft customer. +150% YTD; execution now key differentiator as growth expected to continue.
CEO Dan Sugar proven miracle worker; Cramer's travel trust sold position too early. Strong conviction company with execution track record.

NeoClouds full-stack AI provider; 7x 2030 earnings ($20 EPS). Massive capital access for premium AI compute contracts. Premium deals with Anthropic could unlock stock upside but integration risk remains.
Fantastic last quarter reversed by rotation; now sports 4%+ dividend yield. CEO Ramol Nagua will not accept flat-year stock ahead of Thursday earnings.
$1.8B cap, $700M sales; mini-Quant Services exposure to energy grid upgrades for AI and new energy. Continue higher barring data-center demand collapse.
CEO Brian Nickel turnaround in progress; new protein drink portfolio. Rotation knocked stock to 100; rare buyable dislocation despite ongoing work.

Acquiring Element Solutions for $14.5B (44% ESOL, 56% SLST); arbitrage-driven 15% pullback creates buying opportunity. Nuclear (sold through 2030) + semiconductors + AI data center = critical infrastructure play.
CEO COO donating 2M shares to Trump accounts for eligible children; neocloud operator commanding premium pricing (3–4x standard gigawatt) on Anthropic/Google compute deals.
Last quarter smacked of earnings bottom in beer despite Street negativity; historic rotation seller. Now 3% yield at 130; Mexico World Cup loss already priced.
Prime beneficiary of consumer trade-down; down 20 points from highs. Dual thesis: trade-down shoppers + excess inventory scooped at pennies on dollar from struggling peers.
